steps.title: Area Formula for a Rectangle
steps.content: The area of a rectangle is computed by multiplying its length by its width. With length = 3 and width = 39, the area is 3·39 square units.

steps.title: Identify the Matching Expression
steps.content:
Looking at the choices:
  • Choice A, \$2(3+39)$, is the formula for the perimeter of a rectangle
  • Choice B, \$2(3·39)$, is twice the area
  • Choice C, \$3+39$, is just the sum of the dimensions
  • Choice D, \$3·39$, is exactly the area we calculated
That matches choice D — D is correct.
